title: LY3437943, a novel triple glucagon, GIP, and GLP-1 receptor agonist for glycemic control and weight loss: From discovery to clinical proof of concept
study_design: translational discovery, preclinical pharmacology and early clinical proof of concept
population: preclinical models and early clinical participants
key_results: Established retatrutide as a single peptide agonist at GIP, GLP-1 and glucagon receptors.
Provided the translational foundation for later obesity and type 2 diabetes trials.
safety_findings: Early clinical safety findings were exploratory and not sufficient for long-term safety conclusions.
limitations: Includes preclinical and early clinical evidence; later phase randomised trials govern clinical-effect estimates.
investigational_status_notice: Retatrutide remains investigational and is not approved for public use.
